design_rev2: Rev 2 complete (obs 848 upserted, rev1 history preserved): 15/15 punch-list items done. Run-boundary join implemented (DialectRegistry on RunContext, drain between als.run and session.flush, Promise.allSettled, rejections through existing catch — ADOPTED in ADR-0034 with author-await-only rejected). Thenable #tail queue explicit. Same-path: concurrent = UB documented (sequential-awaited defined via RYOW); PREVENTED overclaim corrected. FIT-18 unawaited-join guard with red-proof. Print containment: #printContained wraps print; print-throw must never reach emit (toAuthoringError would degrade to reason:unknown — gotcha obs 849). §4.4b freezes literal SECURITY.md/.raw/conformance≠safety/two-realms strings. §4.8 sensitive-areas promotion step. Provenance workflow-substring guard. @example on dialect find + 4 kit verbs; ./typescript into FIT-06 PUBLIC_PATHS. Error tails pin {op}+{path}; 'in this run'. SPEC V3 PENDING: 6 deltas (REQ-MC-06 unawaited-join, same-path scoping, REQ-DAS-01 Async usage section, DG-05.1 tail structure, TSD-03.4 'in this run', FIT-06 gate pin) — then owner re-sign, then slice.
design_review_rev1: Council on design rev 1: tech-writer approve-with-notes, security needs-work, architect needs-work (halt-worthy push-back). CONVERGENT ROOT CAUSE: the handle's async completion is not owned by the run boundary — defineFactory awaits only fn's return; an unawaited dialect chain floats → (a) lost edit / stale content race vs run-end flush, (b) throwing .raw becomes unhandledRejection defeating REQ-DG-05 containment (can crash process with ts-morph stack), (c) same-path two-handle clobber — ADR-0034's 'PREVENTED' overclaims: guarantees hold only under unstated unenforced author-await precondition. ARCHITECT FIX (preferred, unanimous direction): outstanding-handles registry on RunContext, drained/awaited before session.flush() — closes all three + makes await optional for correctness. Security blockers: print-at-flush wrap-point misattributed (getter evaluates inside JSON.stringify at emit, off factory frame — getter must wrap print in contained try/catch + flush boundary propagates as run rejection); freeze SECURITY.md/two-realms literal strings in design; sensitive-areas promotion lost AGAIN → §4.8 step. Tech-writer: forgotten-await teaching surface (single @example with await on dialect find + FIT-06 PUBLIC_PATHS gains ./typescript); error tails need {op}+{path} (3 failures currently collapse); 'create it first IN THIS RUN'; async-usage doc section falls through every net (needs REQ-DAS-01 micro-unfreeze). Verified-good: ensureOpen drain detection, lazy-getter serialize-once, no double-print on retry, dryRun laziness, ADR-0033, cap-at-4.
design_note: Design rev 1 complete (obs 848 + design.md): coalescer = Option A handle-owned via ASYNC dialect ops (await chains — the one departure from sync commons verbs; flag for foresight); ensureOpen() re-registration turns global drain into the spec's two-modify split; FIT-17 orphan-guard fitness promoted (council condition met); Option B rejected. ADRs: 0033 (D5), 0034 (coalescer), 0014+0012 amendments — exactly 4. architecture_impact: modifying (exports 3→4, first dependencies entry). ~32 file changes. Test Derivation: 31/31 REQs. .raw() ruled the 2nd distinguishable op — no second structured op. Council review (architect+security+tech-writer) launched on the draft.
spec_phase_note: V1 executor launched (sonnet); council review (BA+QA+security) runs AFTER the draft exists, on the artefact. Orchestrator ruling injected: mid-chain read SPLITS coalescing (QA model) — flush-before-read serializes current AST state into modify#1, later edits become modify#2; non-negotiables: no edit ever lost + RYOW holds for dialect edits. BA's single-modify-across-read reading rejected (would require staging retraction, violating ADR-0015).
phase_status: complete — proposal persisted (obs 843 + proposal.md). Synthesis for spec/design injection: (1) COUNCIL CLASH to pin in spec: QA says mid-chain read SPLITS coalescing into two modifies vs BA C6 says single-modify holds across read — QA's model favored (ADR-0015 staging is non-retractable); spec must pin the author-visible contract explicitly. (2) ARCHITECT CRITICAL on Approach A (executor's pick): mid-chain read ORPHANS the retained directive (Session.flush drains #pending → later edits silently LOST); two-handles-same-path = last-write-wins clobber; halt-condition: A requires a promoted fitness guard (orphan/double-buffer) or B (Session path-keyed thunk registry, ADR-0008 amendment) becomes the honest choice — design decides with both failure-mode tables. (3) SEQUENCING CORRECTION: ts-morph enters LATE (with the real dialect slice), NOT right after S-000; generics + coalescing mechanism proven first with a reusable toy dialect (also the conformance fixture); the toy-dialect vertical is the real walking skeleton, FIT-01 is a guard slice. (4) ADR inventory capped at 4: D5, ADR-0014 amendment, coalescer seam, ADR-0012 amendment — spinning out .raw/FIT-01/naming ADRs = thin-scope violation. (5) Subpath blast radius: FIT-03 own budget (not exemption), FIT-08 + kit-boundary ruling (define-dialect surface = sanctioned dialect-author API), FIT-04 DTS_PAIRS entry, minimum smoke resolve test now (FIT-09 full = Stage 6), scripts learn ./typescript. (6) One-way doors: subpath string, ts-morph as dependency, dialect-author generic seam signatures, conformance guarantee set, .raw closure signature. (7) BA: capabilities C1-C14 (two audiences), SC1-SC8, boundary sentences B1-B7, theatre-criteria catalog for coalescing/error-attribution. (8) PM: followup = stage-5b-dialect-breadth (absorbs 5.3 + primaryPath note; W2/W5 close IN-change); rollback splits at publish boundary (FIT-01 reverts LAST); Intent wording = 'dialect spine with one load-bearing op', recipe-for-future-ASTs claimed as founded bet, not proven.
problem_statement: Who is hurting: schematic authors. What pain: modify is a stub today — no way to mutate an existing source file; authors eject to raw text manipulation, losing type-safety, chaining, and IR fidelity; no predictable recipe for adding future AST libraries. Why now: Stages 1-4 merged — Stage 5 is the O1+O2 convergence proof and the only remaining blocker before Stage 6.
owner_rulings: Subpath: wire ./typescript into package.json#exports NOW via ADR-0014 amendment; name frozen @pbuilder/sdk/typescript (BA rationale: /angular stays free for a future framework op-pack composed via withOps). Full exports map + FIT-09 remain Stage 6.

D5 RATIFIED IN FULL: first dialect = TypeScript via ts-morph as a PLAIN PINNED dependency (not peerDependency). Owner rationale: schematic authors usually work in JS projects and rarely have ts-morph installed; the SDK must come ready to use. Attached security conditions: strict version pin (no wide caret), committed lockfile, npm provenance on publish, leaf isolation via transitive FIT-01. Residual risk documented: two-realms hazard for authors who already use their own ts-morph (their nodes vs our realm inside .raw).
owner_design_inputs: modify content provenance: chained create/copy/move must be visible to modify via Session.read flush-before-read + read-your-own-writes staging (ADR-0015); never a direct client read; commit/discard at run end preserves all-or-nothing
coalescing ↔ flush-seed-rule interaction (Stage 3 design §4.6b) must be treated explicitly in design
coalescing does NOT span a mid-chain read: N edits → read → M edits = TWO modify directives (QA edge) — spec must pin both the coalesced-no-read and split-by-read scenarios
explore_findings_for_design: Council fork → design ADR with weighed alternatives: coalescer mechanism — executor: dialect-handle-only, Session untouched (KIT-02 literal) vs architect: Session gains AST-agnostic pre-flush thunk hook (else run-end flush cannot trigger dialect serialization); either way serialize-before-drain ordering is a fitness candidate

QA halt-conditions: FIT-01 rebuilt as transitive graph-walk + transitive red-proof BEFORE ts-morph lands (S-000); coalescing read-boundary scenarios; executable conformance red-proofs incl. meta planted-violation-fails; ts-morph determinism pins (frozen ManipulationSettings, explicit NewLineKind, no language-service formatter, version-lock gate vs goldens)

Security: extend FIT-05 (serializable-bytes) to the coalesced modify path (create-only today); SECURITY.md needs .raw()-specific verbatim sentence + conformance≠safety caveat, both pinned by REQ-STD-01 guard; sensitive-areas.md rows promote low→medium with concrete paths

BA: 8 draft REQ scenarios (REQ-DIALECT.1-8); .raw-counts-as-second-distinguishable-op question to settle in spec (if QA rules no → removeImport is the candidate second named op); modify-cliff friction doc (create-before-modify, ADR-0017); freeze addImport signature, .raw name, dialect find verb, defineDialect descriptor shape
Executor: FIT-03 commons bundle budget does not cover the new subpath — needs own budget or explicit exemption; find()-only dialect tests need pre-seeded targets (flush-seed rule)
